title: Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Master the classic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with our authentic, easy-to-follow recipe. Get creamy, flavorful results at home!
longTailKeywords: how to make classic butter chicken
butter chicken recipe step by step
h2Headings: How to Make Butter Chicken: Step-by-Step
Tips for the Best Butter Chicken
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of classic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) garnished with fresh cilantro and served with naan bread.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich, creamy flavors of our classic Butter Chicken, a true Indian delight.

description: Experience the rich, creamy, and mildly spiced flavors of classic Butter Chicken, a beloved Indian dish. Tender chicken pieces are simmered in a luscious tomato-based gravy, enriched with butter and cream, creating an unforgettable culinary delight. This authentic Murgh Makhani recipe promises a restaurant-quality experience right in your home kitchen.

ingredients.item: Boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breast

ingredients.notes: roughly chopped, or 1 can (28 oz) crushed tomatoes
soaked in warm water for 15 minutes

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine chicken pieces with yogurt, 1/2 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, Kashmiri red chili powder, turmeric powder, 1/2 tsp garam masala, and 1/2 tsp salt. Mix well to coat all chicken pieces. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours.

instructions.title: Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Melt 1 tbsp butter in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken in a single layer, working in batches if necessary to avoid overcrowding. Cook for 5-7 minutes per side, until browned and cooked through. Remove chicken and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Gravy Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, melt another 2 tbsp butter over medium heat. Add chopped onions and a pinch of salt. Cook until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the remaining 1/2 tbsp ginger-garlic paste and green chilies (if using), and cook for another minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Simmer the Tomato Mixture
instructions.text: Stir in the chopped tomatoes (or crushed tomatoes) and the remaining 1/2 tsp garam masala. Add soaked cashews (drained) and 1 tsp sugar. Bring to a simmer, then cover and cook for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until tomatoes are very soft and broken down.

instructions.title: Blend the Gravy
instructions.text: Carefully transfer the tomato mixture to a blender. Blend until completely smooth. For an extra silky gravy, pass the blended mixture through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pan. This removes any small bits of onion or tomato skin.

instructions.title: Combine and Finish
instructions.text: Return the cooked chicken to the smooth gravy. Stir in the crushed Kasuri Methi and the remaining 1 tbsp butter. Simmer gently for 5 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld. Finally, stir in the heavy cream. Heat through for 1-2 minutes, but do not boil after adding cream.

instructions.title: Serve Hot
instructions.text: Garnish with fresh chopped cilantro. Serve your classic Butter Chicken hot with naan bread, roti, or basmati rice. Enjoy the rich and creamy flavors!

tips: For truly authentic flavor, consider grilling the marinated chicken in a tandoor oven or on a hot grill until slightly charred before adding to the gravy.
Do not skip the Kasuri Methi (dried fenugreek leaves); it provides a distinct, essential flavor to butter chicken. Crush it between your palms before adding to release its aroma.
Adjust the amount of sugar and cream to your preference. Some prefer a sweeter, richer gravy, while others like it more savory. Taste and adjust before serving.
article:
Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani as it's known in its native India, stands as a testament to the country's rich culinary heritage. This iconic dish, with its luscious, creamy tomato gravy and tender pieces of chicken, has captivated palates worldwide, cementing its status as a global favorite. From bustling Delhi eateries to fine-dining establishments across continents, its appeal is undeniable. A symphony of sweet, tangy, and subtly spicy flavors, Butter Chicken offers a comforting and indulgent experience that few other curries can match. Whether you're a seasoned chef or a home cook eager to explore Indian cuisine, mastering this classic recipe is a rewarding journey into the heart of Indian comfort food.

## What is Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani)?

The undisputed king of Indian curries, Butter Chicken, literally translates to "chicken with butter." Its full name, Murgh Makhani, comes from 'murgh' meaning chicken and 'makhani' meaning buttery. Originating in the Moti Mahal restaurant in Delhi in the 1950s, this dish was created by Kundan Lal Gujral as a way to use leftover tandoori chicken. He simmered the grilled chicken in a rich tomato-based sauce, fortified with butter and cream, and flavored with aromatic spices.

At its core, Butter Chicken is characterized by its incredibly smooth, velvety gravy and succulent chicken. The gravy is typically made from pureed tomatoes, onions, cashews (for thickness and richness), and a delicate blend of spices such as garam masala, fenugreek leaves (kasuri methi), and often a touch of sugar to balance the acidity of the tomatoes. The generous addition of butter and cream at various stages gives the dish its signature richness and irresistible texture, making it a truly luxurious meal.

## Ingredients for Classic Butter Chicken

Creating authentic Butter Chicken requires a careful selection of ingredients, each playing a vital role in building its complex flavor profile.

For the Chicken Marinade:
*   500g boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breast, cut into 1-inch pieces
*   1/2 cup plain yogurt
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1 teaspoon red chili powder (Kashmiri for color, regular for heat)
*   1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder
*   1 teaspoon garam masala
*   1 tablespoon lemon juice
*   Salt to taste

For the Gravy:
*   2 tablespoons unsalted butter
*   1 tablespoon oil (vegetable or canola)
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1.5 cups canned crushed tomatoes or 3-4 ripe fresh tomatoes, pureed
*   1/4 cup raw cashews (soaked in warm water for 15 minutes, optional)
*   1 teaspoon red chili powder (Kashmiri for color)
*   1 teaspoon garam masala
*   1/2 teaspoon ground cumin
*   1/2 teaspoon ground coriander
*   1 teaspoon sugar (or to taste, for balancing tanginess)
*   1/2 teaspoon kasuri methi (dried fenugreek leaves), crushed
*   1/2 cup heavy cream (or more, to taste)
*   Salt to taste
*   Water or chicken broth, as needed

For Garnish:
*   Fresh cilantro, chopped
*   A swirl of extra cream

## How to Make Butter Chicken: Step-by-Step

Mastering homemade Butter Chicken is a rewarding culinary journey. Follow these steps for a truly authentic experience:

1.  **Marinate the Chicken:** In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with all the marinade ingredients. Mix well to ensure the chicken is evenly coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or ideally 2-4 hours, to allow the flavors to penetrate and tenderize the chicken.

2.  **Cook the Chicken:** Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, ensuring not to overcrowd the pan. Cook until browned and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes per side. Remove the cooked chicken and set aside.

3.  **Prepare the Gravy Base:** In the same pan, melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.

4.  **Simmer the Gravy Ingredients:** Stir in the crushed tomatoes (or pureed fresh tomatoes), soaked cashews (if using), red chili powder, garam masala, cumin, and coriander. Add a splash of water or broth if the mixture is too thick. Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat, cover, and cook for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the tomatoes have broken down and the sauce has thickened.

5.  **Blend and Strain the Gravy:** Carefully transfer the cooked gravy mixture to a blender. Blend until absolutely smooth. For an extra silky texture, push the blended gravy through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean pot, discarding any solids. This step is crucial for the signature smooth Murgh Makhani gravy.

6.  **Finish the Gravy:** Return the strained gravy to the pot. Stir in the sugar and salt to taste. Bring to a gentle simmer. Add the cooked chicken pieces to the gravy and let them warm through for 5 minutes.

7.  **Add Cream and Kasuri Methi:** Reduce the heat to low. Stir in the heavy cream and the crushed kasuri methi. Simmer for another 2-3 minutes, just until heated through. Do not boil after adding cream, as it can curdle.

8.  **Serve:** Garnish with fresh cilantro and an optional swirl of extra cream.

## Tips for the Best Butter Chicken

Achieving the perfect Butter Chicken involves a few key techniques and considerations:

*   **Marination is Key:** Don't rush the marination process. The longer the chicken marinates, the more flavorful and tender it will be.
*   **Quality Ingredients:** Use good quality butter, fresh cream, and ripe tomatoes for the best flavor. Kashmiri chili powder is recommended for its vibrant red color without excessive heat.
*   **Blend Until Smooth:** The smoothness of the gravy is paramount. A high-speed blender works best. Straining the gravy through a sieve, while optional, elevates the texture to a restaurant-quality level.
*   **Don't Skip Kasuri Methi:** Dried fenugreek leaves (kasuri methi) add a unique, slightly bitter, and aromatic depth that is characteristic of authentic Butter Chicken. Crush them between your palms before adding to release their oils.
*   **Balance Flavors:** Taste and adjust the seasoning. The balance of sweet (from sugar), tangy (from tomatoes), and spicy notes is crucial. A pinch more sugar can cut through the acidity of the tomatoes if needed.
*   **Gentle Simmer:** When adding cream, ensure the heat is low and only simmer gently. High heat can cause the cream to separate or curdle.

## Serving Suggestions for Murgh Makhani

Murgh Makhani is a versatile dish that pairs wonderfully with a variety of accompaniments, enhancing the overall dining experience.

*   **Rice:** The classic pairing is with steamed Basmati rice, allowing the rice to soak up every drop of the rich gravy. Jeera rice (cumin rice) or saffron rice also make excellent choices.
*   **Indian Breads:** No Butter Chicken meal is complete without warm, fluffy Indian breads. Garlic naan, butter naan, tandoori roti, or laccha paratha are perfect for scooping up the luscious sauce.
*   **Sides:** A simple kachumber salad (diced cucumber, tomato, onion with lemon juice and cilantro) provides a refreshing counterpoint to the richness of the curry. A cooling raita (yogurt with cucumber and spices) can also be a delightful addition.
*   **Beverages:** A mango lassi or a simple glass of water can complement the meal beautifully.

## Butter Chicken Variations & FAQs

While the classic Butter Chicken recipe is beloved, there are several variations and common questions that arise:

**Variations:**
*   **Healthier Version:** Reduce the amount of butter and cream, or substitute some cream with plain yogurt for a slightly lighter take.
*   **Spicier Butter Chicken:** Increase the amount of red chili powder or add a fresh green chili for an extra kick.
*   **Smoked Flavor:** For an authentic tandoor-like flavor, you can give the dish a 'dhungar' (charcoal smoking) by placing a small piece of hot charcoal in a bowl in the curry, drizzling ghee over it, and covering the pot for a few minutes.
*   **Vegetarian/Vegan:** Replace chicken with paneer (Indian cheese), tofu, or mixed vegetables for a delicious vegetarian or vegan Makhani.

**FAQs:**
*   **Is Butter Chicken typically spicy?** Traditionally, Butter Chicken is mild to moderately spicy, focusing more on rich, creamy, and tangy flavors. However, the spice level can easily be adjusted to personal preference.
*   **Can I make it ahead of time?** Absolutely! Butter Chicken often tastes even better the next day as the flavors have more time to meld. Store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days.
*   **Can I freeze Butter Chicken?** Yes, you can freeze the gravy without the cream. When ready to serve, thaw, reheat, and then stir in the fresh cream. It can be frozen for up to 2-3 months.
*   **What kind of chicken is best?** Boneless, skinless chicken thighs are often preferred for their tenderness and juiciness, but chicken breast also works well.
*   **Why is my Butter Chicken not red?** The vibrant red color typically comes from using Kashmiri red chili powder, which provides color without much heat, and ripe tomatoes. Some recipes also use a tiny bit of red food coloring, but it's not traditional.

Few dishes capture the heart and palate quite like Butter Chicken. Its rich history, comforting flavors, and luxurious texture make it a staple in Indian cuisine and a global culinary sensation. By following this guide, you can recreate this restaurant-quality dish in your own kitchen, bringing the taste of India right to your dining table. Enjoy the process, savor the aroma, and delight in every creamy, delicious bite of this timeless classic.
